name: CockroachDB Cloud API
what_it_does: CockroachDB Cloud management API for provisioning and managing distributed, PostgreSQL-compatible database clusters with built-in geo-distribution and horizontal scalability.
use_cases: Provisioning and scaling CockroachDB Serverless or Dedicated clusters
Managing database users, roles, and IP allowlists
Retrieving cluster metrics and health status for monitoring
Multi-region cluster configuration for data residency compliance
not_for: OLAP/analytics workloads (use ClickHouse or BigQuery instead)
Teams needing pure self-hosted Postgres without cloud management
best_when: You need a PostgreSQL-compatible distributed database that can scale horizontally and survive regional failures with strong consistency.
avoid_when: You have simple single-region workloads where standard Postgres or managed Postgres (Neon, Supabase) is sufficient.

auth.notes: Service accounts with API keys for Cloud Management API. Database connections use standard PostgreSQL authentication (username/password or client certs). API keys are scoped per service account with role-based permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: CockroachDB Basic: 10 GiB storage, 50M RUs/month free
pricing.notes: Serverless Basic tier is free. Standard and Dedicated tiers for production. RU (Request Unit) pricing for serverless can be hard to predict.

performance.rate_limits: Cloud API: 500 req/min; DB connections: unlimited based on cluster size

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Cloud API returns standard JSON errors with descriptive messages. SQL errors follow PostgreSQL error format with SQLSTATE codes — very informative for agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SQL transactions provide ACID guarantees. Cloud management API operations (create cluster) are not idempotent without checking first.
known_agent_gotchas: Cloud Management API and database SQL connection are separate systems with different auth
CockroachDB has PostgreSQL-like syntax but subtle differences (e.g., sequences, some data types)
Transaction retries are required for serializable isolation — build retry logic into agents
Serverless RU billing can spike unexpectedly with unoptimized queries
Multi-region clusters require explicit region configuration — not automatic
Connection string format differs from standard Postgres (cockroach:// prefix)
